---

## Project Structure Summary: VirtualGfriend (VGfriend / Amoura)

### Overview

VirtualGfriend (internally branded "Amoura") is a Vietnamese-language AI-powered virtual companion application. Users can create AI characters, build relationships over time through chat, complete quests, buy/send gifts, earn achievements, and engage in direct messaging with other users. It is a full-stack monorepo with a **Next.js 14 frontend**, **Express.js backend**, **PostgreSQL** database, **Redis** caching layer, **Socket.io** real-time communication, and **Groq/OpenAI/Gemini** AI integration.

---

### Monorepo Structure

```
VirtualGfriend/
├── client/                # Next.js 14 frontend (App Router)
├── server/                # Express.js backend
├── shared/                # Shared TypeScript types
├── e2e/                   # Playwright E2E tests (API + UI)
├── nginx/                 # Nginx reverse proxy config
├── docs/                  # System documentation (modular)
├── plans/                 # Implementation plans and report templates
├── guide/                 # Reference guides
├── data/                  # (ignored, likely .gitkeep)
├── scripts/               # (ignored, build/deploy scripts)
├── docker-compose.yml     # Production Docker Compose
├── docker-compose.dev.yml # Development Docker Compose (DB only)
└── .env.production.example
```

---

### Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| **Frontend** | Next.js 14 (App Router), React 18, TypeScript, TailwindCSS, Radix UI, Framer Motion, Zustand (state management), Socket.io Client, TanStack React Query |
| **Backend** | Express.js, TypeScript, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL 16, Redis (ioredis), Socket.io, JWT auth, Zod validation, bcryptjs |
| **AI** | Groq (primary), OpenAI, Gemini (via `openai` SDK) |
| **Payments** | Stripe (subscriptions, webhooks) |
| **Email** | Nodemailer (SMTP for OTP/password reset) |
| **Testing** | Playwright (E2E: API + UI, POM pattern, multi-browser) |
| **Infrastructure** | Docker, Docker Compose, Nginx (reverse proxy with SSL), GitHub Actions CI/CD |
| **File Storage** | AWS S3 / DigitalOcean Spaces |

---

### Server (`server/`) -- 76 TypeScript source files

**Entry point:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\server\src\index.ts`

The server bootstraps Express with Helmet, CORS, compression, cookie-parser, rate limiting (separate public and per-user limiters), health/readiness endpoints, and Socket.io with JWT auth middleware. It runs periodic cleanup jobs for expired refresh tokens (every 6h), soft-deleted DMs (every 24h), and leaderboard cache (every 5m).

**Modules (17 feature modules):**

| Module | Files | Purpose |
|--------|-------|---------|
| `auth/` | auth.controller, auth.service, auth.routes, password-reset.controller, password-reset.service | Registration, login, JWT, refresh tokens, OTP, password reset |
| `users/` | users.controller, users.service, users.routes | User profile management |
| `character/` | character.controller, character.service, character.routes, facts.controller, mood.service, relationship.controller, relationship.service, template.controller, template.service | Character CRUD, relationship stages, facts learning, mood tracking, templates |
| `chat/` | chat.controller, chat.service, chat.routes | AI chat message handling |
| `ai/` | ai.service, conversation-summary.service, facts-learning.service, proactive-notification.service | AI integration, context management, proactive notifications |
| `dm/` | dm.controller, dm.service, dm.routes | Direct messaging between users |
| `quest/` | quest.controller, quest.service, quest.routes | Quest system (daily, weekly, story, achievement, event) |
| `gift/` | gift.controller, gift.service, gift.routes | Gift shop, purchase, send |
| `scene/` | scene.controller, scene.service, scene.routes | Environment/scene unlocking |
| `memory/` | memory.controller, memory.service, memory.routes, auto-memory.service | Memory gallery, auto-generated conversation summaries |
| `game/` | game-event.service, game.routes | Gamification events |
| `leaderboard/` | leaderboard.controller, leaderboard.service, leaderboard.routes | Rankings by level, affection, streak |
| `analytics/` | analytics.controller, analytics.service, analytics.routes | Usage analytics |
| `payment/` | payment.controller, payment.service, payment.routes, payment.constants | Stripe subscriptions, webhooks |
| `admin/` | admin.controller, admin.routes, admin.middleware, admin-pricing.controller, admin-tier-config.controller, tier-config.service | Admin panel, tier configuration |
| `upload/` | upload.routes, upload.service | File upload (S3/Spaces) |
| `monitoring/` | telemetry.service | System telemetry and metrics |

**Middleware:** `auth.middleware.ts`, `error.middleware.ts`, `premium.middleware.ts`, `request-id.middleware.ts`, `request-monitoring.middleware.ts`

**Library:** `prisma.ts`, `redis.ts`, `logger.ts`, `email.ts`, `stripe.ts`, `constants.ts`

**Routes index:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\server\src\routes\index.ts` -- mounts all module routes under `/api/*`:
- `/api/auth`, `/api/users`, `/api/character`, `/api/chat`, `/api/quests`, `/api/gifts`, `/api/shop`, `/api/scenes`, `/api/memories`, `/api/game`, `/api/analytics`, `/api/dm`, `/api/leaderboard`, `/api/admin`, `/api/payment`, `/api/config/tier-plans`

**Socket.io handlers:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\server\src\sockets\index.ts` -- handles `message:send`, `message:receive`, `character:typing`, `character:mood_change`, `character:affection_change`, `dm:send`, `dm:receive`, `dm:typing`, `dm:read`, `sync:request/response`, `notification:proactive`, with rate limiting, idempotency (dedup keys via Redis), and cross-tab room broadcasting.

**Database schema:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\server\prisma\schema.prisma` -- 930 lines covering: User, RefreshToken, UserSettings, CharacterTemplate, Character, CharacterFact, CharacterScene, RelationshipHistory, Message, Quest, UserQuest, Gift, UserGift, GiftHistory, Scene, Memory, ConversationSummary, Achievement, UserAchievement, Notification, DailyReward, SystemConfig, MonitoringEvent, MonitoringMetricRollup, AIPromptTemplate, PasswordResetOTP, PasswordResetToken, plus Stripe (Subscription, PaymentHistory) and DM (Conversation, ConversationMember, DirectMessage) models. Enums include PremiumTier (FREE/BASIC/PRO/ULTIMATE), RelationshipStage (STRANGER through LOVER), Gender, QuestType, Rarity, etc.

**Database seed:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\server\prisma\seed.ts`

---

### Client (`client/`) -- 83 TypeScript/TSX source files

**Entry point:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\client\src\app\layout.tsx`

**Framework:** Next.js 14 App Router, output: `standalone` (Docker-friendly)

**Styling:** TailwindCSS with dark mode, custom "love" theme (#ad2bee purple), Radix UI components, Framer Motion animations, Plus Jakarta Sans font with Vietnamese support

**Pages (27 routes):**
- Public: `/`, `/about`, `/features`, `/pricing`, `/reviews`, `/blog`, `/blog/[slug]`, `/careers`, `/contact`, `/help`, `/terms`, `/privacy`
- Auth: `/auth/login`, `/auth/register`, `/auth/forgot-password`, `/auth/verify-otp`, `/auth/reset-password`
- App: `/dashboard`, `/chat`, `/onboarding`, `/messages`, `/memories`, `/quests`, `/shop`, `/shop/inventory`, `/leaderboard`, `/admin`, `/analytics`, `/subscription`, `/payment/success`, `/payment/cancel`
- Settings: `/settings`, `/settings/profile`, `/settings/character`, `/settings/appearance`, `/settings/facts`, `/settings/language`, `/settings/about`, `/settings/help`, `/settings/privacy`

**State management (8 Zustand stores):**
- `auth-store.ts` -- JWT tokens, user state
- `character-store.ts` -- Character data, mood, affection
- `chat-store.ts` -- Messages, typing state
- `notification-store.ts` -- Popups, level-up, relationship upgrade, quests
- `premium-store.ts` -- Premium tier state
- `scene-store.ts` -- Active scene
- `language-store.ts` -- i18n

**Services:**
- `api.ts` -- ApiClient class with GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE, automatic token refresh on 401, rate limit retry on 429, BroadcastChannel cross-tab token sync
- `socket.ts` -- SocketService singleton with JWT auth, reconnection, external listener re-registration, cross-tab sync via Socket.io rooms

**Hooks:** `use-auth-guard.ts`, `use-toast.ts`

**Components:** UI primitives (avatar, button, card, input, progress, tabs, toast), notifications (affection-popup, level-up-modal, proactive-notification, relationship-upgrade-modal), layout (app-layout, static-page-layout), PremiumGate, providers, error boundaries, analytics dashboard, facts manager, ad banner

---

### Shared Types (`shared/`)

**File:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\shared\src\types\index.ts`

Comprehensive TypeScript interfaces for User, Auth, Character, CharacterFact, Message, Quest, UserQuest, Gift, UserGift, GiftHistory, Scene, Memory, Achievement, Notification, API responses (paginated, error), and AI context/response. These are shared between client and server via path aliases (`@shared/*`).

---

### E2E Tests (`e2e/`) -- Playwright with Page Object Model

**Configuration:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\playwright.config.ts` -- multi-project (API, Chromium, Firefox, WebKit, Mobile Chrome/Safari), global setup/teardown, auto-starts dev servers, traces/screenshots/video on failure, retry on CI

**API Tests (4 spec files):**
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\api\auth.spec.ts` -- register, login, logout, password reset, health check
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\api\character.spec.ts`
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\api\chat.spec.ts`
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\api\features.spec.ts`

**UI Tests (6 spec files):**
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\ui\auth\login.spec.ts` -- login page elements, validation, invalid credentials, successful login, session persistence
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\ui\auth\register.spec.ts`
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\ui\auth\password-reset.spec.ts`
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\ui\chat\chat.spec.ts`
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\ui\dashboard\dashboard.spec.ts`
- `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\ui\onboarding\onboarding.spec.ts`

**Page Objects (8):** `BasePage.ts`, `LoginPage.ts`, `RegisterPage.ts`, `ChatPage.ts`, `DashboardPage.ts`, `OnboardingPage.ts`, `PasswordResetPages.ts`, `index.ts`

**Test utilities:** `helpers.ts`, `test-data.ts`, `index.ts`

**Fixtures:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\e2e\tests\fixtures.ts`

---

### Key Architectural Patterns

1. **Cache-aside pattern** -- Redis cache in front of PostgreSQL for user auth data, conversation members, character lists, leaderboard
2. **Idempotency via Redis** -- `setNX` dedup keys prevent duplicate message/DM processing on retries
3. **Cross-tab synchronization** -- Socket.io rooms for real-time sync across browser tabs, BroadcastChannel for token sync
4. **Rate limiting** -- Dual strategy: IP-based for public routes (auth), per-user (JWT-decoded) for authenticated routes; also socket-level rate limiting
5. **Premium gating** -- Four-tier system (FREE/BASIC/PRO/ULTIMATE) enforced via middleware and database fields
6. **Relationship progression** -- 8-stage affinity system (STRANGER -> LOVER) with milestone tracking, history logging, and unlock gating
7. **Graceful shutdown** -- SIGTERM handler disconnects Redis and Prisma, then closes HTTP server
8. **Periodic cleanup** -- Interval-based jobs for token expiration, DM soft-delete purge, leaderboard cache invalidation

---

### Docker & Deployment

- **Production:** `docker-compose.yml` -- PostgreSQL, Redis, Server, Client, Nginx (production profile), all with healthchecks, all images from GHCR
- **Development:** `docker-compose.dev.yml` -- PostgreSQL, Redis, Adminer (DB UI at port 8080)
- **Nginx:** `C:\Users\ChuHai\Documents\VirtualGfriend\nginx\nginx.conf` -- reverse proxy with HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect, WebSocket upgrade support for Socket.io

I now have a comprehensive view of the entire backend codebase. Let me compile the bug hunt report.

---

# Comprehensive Backend Bug Hunt Report

**Date:** 2026-04-09
**Scope:** `server/src/` — 76 TypeScript files, Prisma schema

---

## CRITICAL BUGS

### BUG-1: Race Condition — Double-Spend in Quest Reward Claiming
**Files:** `server/src/modules/quest/quest.service.ts` (claimReward), `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` (autoClaimQuest)
**Severity:** Critical
**Description:** The `claimReward` method and `autoClaimQuest` can both claim the same quest simultaneously. While `claimReward` uses `$transaction` to update status + give currencies, `autoClaimQuest` (called from `processAction`) does the exact same thing independently. If a user triggers a quest completion via HTTP call to `claimReward` at the same time the game event system auto-claims it, both transactions read `status === 'COMPLETED'` and both proceed to award rewards.
**Evidence:** `autoClaimQuest` at game-event.service.ts reads the quest, checks `status !== 'COMPLETED'`, then updates — this is a classic TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race.
**Fix:** Use atomic `updateMany` with a status guard: `tx.userQuest.updateMany({ where: { id, status: 'COMPLETED' }, data: { status: 'CLAIMED' } })` and check `result.count === 0` to detect races.

### BUG-2: Memory Leak — Unbounded `rateLimiters` Map in Socket Handlers
**File:** `server/src/sockets/index.ts`
**Severity:** Critical
**Description:** The `rateLimiters` Map stores per-user-per-event entries that are never cleaned up. Each entry is keyed as `${userId}:${eventType}`. Over time, as users connect/disconnect, the map grows without bound. A server with many ephemeral users will accumulate stale entries indefinitely.
**Fix:** Add a periodic cleanup interval (e.g., every 5 minutes) that deletes entries where `resetAt < Date.now()`.

### BUG-3: Missing Auth Check — Webhook Endpoint Can Be Abused
**File:** `server/src/index.ts` (line ~68), `server/src/modules/payment/payment.controller.ts`
**Severity:** Critical
**Description:** The Stripe webhook is mounted at `/api/payment/webhook` with `express.raw({ type: 'application/json' })` BEFORE `express.json()` middleware. However, the route is mounted through the main `app.post()` call, which sits BEFORE `app.use('/api', router)`. If an attacker sends a POST to `/api/payment/webhook` with a crafted `stripe-signature` header and the `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` is missing, the response is `400` with `{ error: 'Missing signature or webhook secret' }` — this leaks the fact that the webhook secret is missing, aiding reconnaissance.
**Fix:** Return a generic 400 response regardless of whether the secret is missing. Also ensure the webhook endpoint is not behind any other middleware that might interfere with the raw body.

---

## HIGH SEVERITY BUGS

### BUG-4: SQL Injection Risk via Raw Query Parameter Interpolation
**File:** `server/src/modules/users/users.service.ts` (getNotifications)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** The raw SQL query uses template literals for `skip` and `safeLimit` values: `OFFSET ${skip}` and `LIMIT ${safeLimit}`. While Prisma's `$queryRaw` does parameterize `userId`, numeric literals passed directly into OFFSET/LIMIT are technically safe from SQL injection. However, the `regex pattern` in the WHERE clause `'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}T'` uses double-escaped backslashes which may not work as intended in all Prisma versions. The real issue: the `expiresAt` check uses `::timestamptz` cast which will throw a PostgreSQL error if the JSON contains a non-date string, causing the entire query to fail for that user.
**Fix:** Wrap the JSON date extraction in a `CASE WHEN` with a try-catch pattern, or filter expired notifications in application code after fetching.

### BUG-5: Inconsistent Password Validation Between Controller and Service
**Files:** `server/src/modules/auth/auth.controller.ts` (changePasswordSchema), `server/src/modules/auth/auth.service.ts` (changePassword), `server/src/lib/constants.ts`
**Severity:** High
**Description:** `changePasswordSchema` in the controller requires `min(6)` characters, but `validatePassword` in constants.ts requires 8+ characters, uppercase, lowercase, number, AND special character. The controller's Zod schema will allow a 6-character password through, which will then fail the service's validation — but the error message will come from the service, not the schema, creating a confusing UX. Worse, the `resetPasswordSchema` in `password-reset.controller.ts` also uses `min(6)`, meaning a user can reset their password to something weaker than what was required at registration.
**Fix:** Align all schemas to use the same validation constants. Use `validatePassword` output to generate Zod refinements.

### BUG-6: Stale Cache After User Update in `attachPremiumInfo`
**File:** `server/src/middlewares/premium.middleware.ts` (attachPremiumInfo)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** `attachPremiumInfo` queries the database directly (bypassing the auth middleware's cache) and doesn't update the cache after auto-downgrading expired users. This means the next request using `getUserFromCacheOrDb` in the auth middleware will get stale data showing the user still has premium access. The auto-downgrade is written to DB but the cache still holds the old premium state.
**Fix:** After auto-downgrading, also delete the cache key: `await cache.del(CacheKeys.userAuth(userId), CacheKeys.user(userId))`.

### BUG-7: Console.log Leaks Sensitive Data in Production
**Files:** `server/src/modules/auth/auth.service.ts` (login method)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** The `login` method contains three `console.log` statements that output login attempt details, user found status, and password check results. These bypass the structured logger and will appear in production logs, potentially exposing authentication patterns. The `console.log('[Auth] Login attempt:', ...)` includes email addresses.
**Fix:** Replace all `console.log` with the structured `log.debug()` calls from `createModuleLogger`.

### BUG-8: Missing Transaction in `relationshipService.reconcileRelationship`
**File:** `server/src/modules/character/relationship.service.ts`
**Severity:** High
**Description:** The `reconcileRelationship` function performs multiple database operations (updateMany to deactivate current relationships, update to reactivate the old one, update to deduct gems, create event record) without wrapping them in a transaction. If any operation fails mid-way (e.g., gem deduction fails), the system ends up in an inconsistent state where the old character is reactivated but gems aren't deducted, or vice versa.
**Fix:** Wrap all operations in `prisma.$transaction()`.

### BUG-9: N+1 Query Pattern in Leaderboard Service
**File:** `server/src/modules/leaderboard/leaderboard.service.ts` (getByAchievements)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** The `getByAchievements` method first does a `groupBy` query to get user IDs and counts, then does a second `findMany` to get user details. While this avoids N+1, the `distinct: ['userId']` queries in `getByLevel` and `getByAffection` include a nested `user` relation which Prisma handles as a separate query per result in some database configurations, effectively creating an N+1 pattern.
**Fix:** Use raw SQL or split the query: first get character IDs ordered by level/affection, then batch-fetch users with a single `WHERE id IN (...)` query.

### BUG-10: Unhandled Promise Rejection in Background Tasks
**Files:** `server/src/modules/chat/chat.service.ts` (sendMessage)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** Multiple background operations are spawned with `.catch()` handlers that only log errors but never propagate them. While this is intentional for non-blocking behavior, the inline fact saving, auto-memory creation, and facts extraction all share a pattern where failures are silently swallowed. If the database is temporarily unavailable, facts and memories are silently lost without any retry mechanism or dead-letter queue.
**Specific instances:**
- `autoMemoryService.createRelationshipStageMemory(...).catch(...)` 
- `autoMemoryService.checkAffinityMilestone(...).catch(...)`
- `autoMemoryService.createLevelUpMemory(...).catch(...)`
- `factsLearningService.extractAndSaveFacts(...).catch(...)`
- `conversationSummaryService.createSummary(...).catch(...)`
**Fix:** Implement a retry queue with exponential backoff for background tasks.

---

## MEDIUM SEVERITY BUGS

### BUG-11: Duplicate Reward Distribution — `autoClaimQuest` Calls `processAction` Recursively
**File:** `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` (autoClaimQuest)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** When `autoClaimQuest` completes a quest, it calls `processAction` with `action: 'COMPLETE_QUEST'` recursively. If there's a meta-quest that triggers on `COMPLETE_QUEST`, it could trigger another quest completion, which calls `autoClaimQuest` again, creating a cascade. The recursion guard (`MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH = 3`) limits this but the logic can still distribute unintended rewards through meta-quest chains.
**Fix:** Separate the reward distribution from the meta-quest triggering. Only trigger meta-quests for user-initiated actions, not auto-claimed rewards.

### BUG-12: Redis Counter Drift for Message/Gift Milestone Counts
**File:** `server/src/modules/chat/chat.service.ts` (sendMessage), `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` (checkMilestones)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `msg_count:${userId}:${characterId}` Redis counter is used to avoid expensive `COUNT(*)` queries. However, if the Redis counter is lost (e.g., Redis restart, TTL expiry), the fallback uses `COUNT(*)` and caches the result — but the counter in the chat service's own flow is incremented independently. This means the counter can drift from the actual DB count, causing milestone checks to fire at wrong times (e.g., milestone at 100 messages might fire at 95 or 105).
**Fix:** Periodically reconcile Redis counters with actual DB counts, or use Redis `INCR` atomically alongside DB writes in a transaction with a post-commit hook.

### BUG-13: Missing Zod Validation on DM Controller
**File:** `server/src/modules/dm/dm.controller.ts`
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** Unlike other controllers, the DM controller has no Zod schema validation. The `sendMessage` method directly reads `content` and `messageType` from `req.body` without validation. A malicious client could send non-string content, extremely long strings, or invalid message types that could cause downstream issues.
**Fix:** Add Zod schemas for all DM controller endpoints matching the service's expectations.

### BUG-14: Missing Input Sanitization in Character Fact Keys
**Files:** `server/src/modules/character/facts.controller.ts` (addFact), `server/src/modules/character/character.service.ts` (addFact)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `addFact` endpoint in facts.controller.ts accepts user-defined `key` values up to 100 characters without normalization. The `character.service.ts` `addFact` method also doesn't normalize the key. However, the AI service's inline fact extraction normalizes keys to `snake_case`. This inconsistency means the same logical fact could be stored under different keys (e.g., `"user's favorite color"` vs `"user_s_favorite_color"`), causing duplicate/conflicting facts.
**Fix:** Normalize fact keys in the service layer using the same normalization function used by the AI inline fact extraction.

### BUG-15: Incorrect HTTP Status Code for Registration
**File:** `server/src/modules/auth/auth.controller.ts` (register)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `register` endpoint returns `200 OK` when it should return `202 Accepted` or `201 Created`. The OTP has been sent but the user account isn't created yet (that happens in `verifyRegistration`). Returning 200 is misleading as it implies the operation is complete.
**Fix:** Return `202 Accepted` with a message indicating OTP verification is pending.

### BUG-16: Missing `characterId` Ownership Check in Scene Service
**File:** `server/src/modules/scene/scene.service.ts` (unlockScene, setActiveScene)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** While the service checks that the user owns the active character (`prisma.character.findFirst({ where: { userId, isActive: true } })`), it then uses `character.id` for scene operations. If a user somehow has no active character, the `!character` check catches it. However, if a user has multiple characters (premium users), only the `isActive: true` one is checked — but scene unlocks are tied to a specific `characterId`. If the user deactivates their current character and activates another, the previously unlocked scenes don't transfer, but there's no user feedback about this.
**Fix:** This is more a UX issue than a security issue, but consider documenting this behavior or adding a warning.

### BUG-17: Potential XSS via Unescaped User Content in Email Templates
**File:** `server/src/lib/email.ts`
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The email templates interpolate the `otp` variable directly into HTML: `${otp}`. While OTPs are generated as numeric strings (6 digits), if the OTP generation logic ever changes to include user-controlled input, it would be an XSS vector. Currently safe due to `crypto.randomInt(100000, 999999).toString()`, but the pattern is fragile.
**Fix:** Use HTML entity encoding for all interpolated values in email templates, or use a template engine that auto-escapes.

### BUG-18: Race Condition in Daily Quest Auto-Start
**File:** `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` (autoStartDailyQuests)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The debounce uses a Redis key with TTL until midnight. If multiple server instances are running (horizontal scaling), the Redis debounce works correctly. However, the `deleteMany` + `create` operations happen in a transaction that doesn't account for the race where one instance deletes old progress and another instance is simultaneously incrementing progress on those same quests. The deleted progress is lost.
**Fix:** Only delete quests that are expired (started before yesterday), not just "before today". Or use an upsert pattern.

### BUG-19: Missing Error Response Format Consistency
**Files:** Multiple controllers
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** Some controllers return `{ success: false, error: { code, message } }` format (via AppError + error middleware), while others return `{ success: false, message: string }` directly (e.g., `password-reset.controller.ts`). This inconsistency makes it harder for frontend clients to handle errors uniformly.
**Specific files:**
- `password-reset.controller.ts` — returns `{ success: false, message: ... }` directly
- `auth.controller.ts` — uses AppError (correct pattern)
- `dm.controller.ts` — throws AppError (correct)
**Fix:** Standardize all error responses through the AppError + error middleware pattern.

### BUG-20: `getActiveCharacter` in Chat Controller Doesn't Validate Character ID
**File:** `server/src/modules/chat/chat.controller.ts` (getHistory)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `getHistory` endpoint silently returns an empty message list if the user has no active character, rather than returning a 404 or a proper "no character" error. This could confuse clients that expect an error when no character exists.
**Fix:** Return a proper error response or at least include a flag in the response indicating no active character exists.

---

## LOW SEVERITY BUGS

### BUG-21: Hardcoded Redis URL Falls Back Without Warning
**File:** `server/src/lib/redis.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** If `REDIS_URL` is not set, the code falls back to `redis://localhost:6379` silently. In a Docker compose or production environment where Redis is on a different host, this causes silent connection failures. The warning log only fires if the connection fails, not when the default is used.
**Fix:** Log a warning when falling back to the default Redis URL.

### BUG-22: Unused `isVipTier` Import in Premium Middleware
**File:** `server/src/middlewares/premium.middleware.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The file imports `isVipTier` from `../lib/constants` but the function is also defined in `constants.ts` with a different implementation. The imported version checks `tier !== 'FREE'`, which is correct, but the duplication creates maintenance confusion.

### BUG-23: Magic Number in Socket Timeout
**File:** `server/src/sockets/index.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The AI response typing delay uses `setTimeout(() => {...}, 1000 + Math.random() * 2000)` — the 1000-3000ms range is hardcoded. The constants file has `TIMINGS.AI_TYPING_DELAY = 1500` but it's not used here.
**Fix:** Use the constant value or add min/max timing constants.

### BUG-24: Missing `distinct` Support May Cause Incorrect Leaderboard Results
**File:** `server/src/modules/leaderboard/leaderboard.service.ts` (getByLevel, getByAffection)
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The `distinct: ['userId']` in Prisma queries with `take: limit` may not work as expected on PostgreSQL when combined with `orderBy`. Prisma's `distinct` with ordering on different columns can produce unexpected results — it may return more than `limit` rows or skip legitimate top entries.
**Fix:** Use a subquery or raw SQL to get the best character per user first, then order and limit.

### BUG-25: `healthCache` Never Expires in Memory
**File:** `server/src/index.ts` (health endpoint)
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The health check uses an in-memory `healthCache` variable with a 5-second TTL. This works correctly but if the server runs for months without restart, this object persists indefinitely (albeit small). More importantly, in a multi-instance setup behind a load balancer, each instance maintains its own cache, causing inconsistent health responses.
**Fix:** Not a real issue for single-instance, but worth noting for horizontal scaling.

### BUG-26: Prisma Client Global Reuse in Development Could Mask Connection Issues
**File:** `server/src/lib/prisma.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The `global.prisma` pattern is correct for hot-reloading in development, but the connection logging (`prisma.$connect().then(...).catch(...)`) runs on every module reload in dev mode, potentially creating multiple connection pools. In dev mode with hot-reload, this could exhaust the database connection pool.
**Fix:** Only call `$connect()` explicitly; Prisma auto-connects on first query. Or guard the connection attempt.

### BUG-27: Missing `isActive` Filter in Gift Service's `sendGift`
**File:** `server/src/modules/gift/gift.service.ts` (sendGift)
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The `sendGift` method checks character ownership with `prisma.character.findFirst({ where: { id: data.characterId, userId } })` but doesn't filter by `isActive: true`. A user could theoretically send a gift to an ended/inactive character, which would still create gift history and AI responses.
**Fix:** Add `isActive: true` to the character ownership check.

### BUG-28: `updateCharacter` in Character Controller Doesn't Validate Response Style/Creativity Level Values
**File:** `server/src/modules/character/character.controller.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The `updateCharacterSchema` allows `responseStyle` as any string and `creativityLevel` as any number 0-1. However, the Character model's `responseStyle` has a default of `"romantic"` with no enum constraint, and `creativityLevel` is a Float. While technically valid, accepting arbitrary strings for `responseStyle` could cause issues if the AI service expects specific values.
**Fix:** Add enum validation for `responseStyle` matching the values the AI service actually uses.

### BUG-29: `getSubscriptionStatus` Makes Two Separate Queries When One Would Suffice
**File:** `server/src/modules/payment/payment.service.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** `getSubscriptionStatus` queries `prisma.subscription` and `prisma.user` separately. These could be combined or the user query could include the subscription relation.
**Fix:** Use `prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { id: userId }, include: { subscription: true } })` to fetch both in one query.

### BUG-30: Graceful Shutdown Doesn't Drain Socket Connections
**File:** `server/src/index.ts` (SIGTERM handler)

**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `DirectMessage` model has `@@index([conversationId, createdAt])` and `@@index([conversationId, isDeleted, createdAt])`, which is good. ---

# Comprehensive Backend Bug Hunt Report

**Date:** 2026-04-09
**Scope:** `server/src/` — 76 TypeScript files, Prisma schema

---

## CRITICAL BUGS

### BUG-1: Race Condition — Double-Spend in Quest Reward Claiming
**Files:** `server/src/modules/quest/quest.service.ts` (claimReward), `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` (autoClaimQuest)
**Severity:** Critical
**Description:** The `claimReward` method and `autoClaimQuest` can both claim the same quest simultaneously. While `claimReward` uses `$transaction` to update status + give currencies, `autoClaimQuest` (called from `processAction`) does the exact same thing independently. If a user triggers a quest completion via HTTP call to `claimReward` at the same time the game event system auto-claims it, both transactions read `status === 'COMPLETED'` and both proceed to award rewards.
**Evidence:** `autoClaimQuest` at game-event.service.ts reads the quest, checks `status !== 'COMPLETED'`, then updates — this is a classic TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) race.
**Fix:** Use atomic `updateMany` with a status guard: `tx.userQuest.updateMany({ where: { id, status: 'COMPLETED' }, data: { status: 'CLAIMED' } })` and check `result.count === 0` to detect races.

### BUG-2: Memory Leak — Unbounded `rateLimiters` Map in Socket Handlers
**File:** `server/src/sockets/index.ts`
**Severity:** Critical
**Description:** The `rateLimiters` Map stores per-user-per-event entries that are never cleaned up. Each entry is keyed as `${userId}:${eventType}`. Over time, as users connect/disconnect, the map grows without bound. A server with many ephemeral users will accumulate stale entries indefinitely.
**Fix:** Add a periodic cleanup interval (e.g., every 5 minutes) that deletes entries where `resetAt < Date.now()`.

### BUG-3: Missing Auth Check — Webhook Endpoint Can Be Abused
**File:** `server/src/index.ts` (line ~68), `server/src/modules/payment/payment.controller.ts`
**Severity:** Critical
**Description:** The Stripe webhook is mounted at `/api/payment/webhook` with `express.raw({ type: 'application/json' })` BEFORE `express.json()` middleware. However, the route is mounted through the main `app.post()` call, which sits BEFORE `app.use('/api', router)`. If an attacker sends a POST to `/api/payment/webhook` with a crafted `stripe-signature` header and the `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` is missing, the response is `400` with `{ error: 'Missing signature or webhook secret' }` — this leaks the fact that the webhook secret is missing, aiding reconnaissance.
**Fix:** Return a generic 400 response regardless of whether the secret is missing. Also ensure the webhook endpoint is not behind any other middleware that might interfere with the raw body.

---

## HIGH SEVERITY BUGS

### BUG-4: SQL Injection Risk via Raw Query Parameter Interpolation
**File:** `server/src/modules/users/users.service.ts` (getNotifications)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** The raw SQL query uses template literals for `skip` and `safeLimit` values: `OFFSET ${skip}` and `LIMIT ${safeLimit}`. While Prisma's `$queryRaw` does parameterize `userId`, numeric literals passed directly into OFFSET/LIMIT are technically safe from SQL injection. However, the `regex pattern` in the WHERE clause `'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}T'` uses double-escaped backslashes which may not work as intended in all Prisma versions. The real issue: the `expiresAt` check uses `::timestamptz` cast which will throw a PostgreSQL error if the JSON contains a non-date string, causing the entire query to fail for that user.
**Fix:** Wrap the JSON date extraction in a `CASE WHEN` with a try-catch pattern, or filter expired notifications in application code after fetching.

### BUG-5: Inconsistent Password Validation Between Controller and Service
**Files:** `server/src/modules/auth/auth.controller.ts` (changePasswordSchema), `server/src/modules/auth/auth.service.ts` (changePassword), `server/src/lib/constants.ts`
**Severity:** High
**Description:** `changePasswordSchema` in the controller requires `min(6)` characters, but `validatePassword` in constants.ts requires 8+ characters, uppercase, lowercase, number, AND special character. The controller's Zod schema will allow a 6-character password through, which will then fail the service's validation — but the error message will come from the service, not the schema, creating a confusing UX. Worse, the `resetPasswordSchema` in `password-reset.controller.ts` also uses `min(6)`, meaning a user can reset their password to something weaker than what was required at registration.
**Fix:** Align all schemas to use the same validation constants. Use `validatePassword` output to generate Zod refinements.

### BUG-6: Stale Cache After User Update in `attachPremiumInfo`
**File:** `server/src/middlewares/premium.middleware.ts` (attachPremiumInfo)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** `attachPremiumInfo` queries the database directly (bypassing the auth middleware's cache) and doesn't update the cache after auto-downgrading expired users. This means the next request using `getUserFromCacheOrDb` in the auth middleware will get stale data showing the user still has premium access. The auto-downgrade is written to DB but the cache still holds the old premium state.
**Fix:** After auto-downgrading, also delete the cache key: `await cache.del(CacheKeys.userAuth(userId), CacheKeys.user(userId))`.

### BUG-7: Console.log Leaks Sensitive Data in Production
**Files:** `server/src/modules/auth/auth.service.ts` (login method)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** The `login` method contains three `console.log` statements that output login attempt details, user found status, and password check results. These bypass the structured logger and will appear in production logs, potentially exposing authentication patterns. The `console.log('[Auth] Login attempt:', ...)` includes email addresses.
**Fix:** Replace all `console.log` with the structured `log.debug()` calls from `createModuleLogger`.

### BUG-8: Missing Transaction in `relationshipService.reconcileRelationship`
**File:** `server/src/modules/character/relationship.service.ts`
**Severity:** High
**Description:** The `reconcileRelationship` function performs multiple database operations (updateMany to deactivate current relationships, update to reactivate the old one, update to deduct gems, create event record) without wrapping them in a transaction. If any operation fails mid-way (e.g., gem deduction fails), the system ends up in an inconsistent state where the old character is reactivated but gems aren't deducted, or vice versa.
**Fix:** Wrap all operations in `prisma.$transaction()`.

### BUG-9: N+1 Query Pattern in Leaderboard Service
**File:** `server/src/modules/leaderboard/leaderboard.service.ts` (getByAchievements)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** The `getByAchievements` method first does a `groupBy` query to get user IDs and counts, then does a second `findMany` to get user details. While this avoids N+1, the `distinct: ['userId']` queries in `getByLevel` and `getByAffection` include a nested `user` relation which Prisma handles as a separate query per result in some database configurations, effectively creating an N+1 pattern.
**Fix:** Use raw SQL or split the query: first get character IDs ordered by level/affection, then batch-fetch users with a single `WHERE id IN (...)` query.

### BUG-10: Unhandled Promise Rejection in Background Tasks
**Files:** `server/src/modules/chat/chat.service.ts` (sendMessage)
**Severity:** High
**Description:** Multiple background operations are spawned with `.catch()` handlers that only log errors but never propagate them. While this is intentional for non-blocking behavior, the inline fact saving, auto-memory creation, and facts extraction all share a pattern where failures are silently swallowed. If the database is temporarily unavailable, facts and memories are silently lost without any retry mechanism or dead-letter queue.
**Specific instances:**
- `autoMemoryService.createRelationshipStageMemory(...).catch(...)` 
- `autoMemoryService.checkAffinityMilestone(...).catch(...)`
- `autoMemoryService.createLevelUpMemory(...).catch(...)`
- `factsLearningService.extractAndSaveFacts(...).catch(...)`
- `conversationSummaryService.createSummary(...).catch(...)`
**Fix:** Implement a retry queue with exponential backoff for background tasks.

---

## MEDIUM SEVERITY BUGS

### BUG-11: Duplicate Reward Distribution — `autoClaimQuest` Calls `processAction` Recursively
**File:** `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` (autoClaimQuest)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** When `autoClaimQuest` completes a quest, it calls `processAction` with `action: 'COMPLETE_QUEST'` recursively. If there's a meta-quest that triggers on `COMPLETE_QUEST`, it could trigger another quest completion, which calls `autoClaimQuest` again, creating a cascade. The recursion guard (`MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH = 3`) limits this but the logic can still distribute unintended rewards through meta-quest chains.
**Fix:** Separate the reward distribution from the meta-quest triggering. Only trigger meta-quests for user-initiated actions, not auto-claimed rewards.

### BUG-12: Redis Counter Drift for Message/Gift Milestone Counts
**File:** `server/src/modules/chat/chat.service.ts` (sendMessage), `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` (checkMilestones)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `msg_count:${userId}:${characterId}` Redis counter is used to avoid expensive `COUNT(*)` queries. However, if the Redis counter is lost (e.g., Redis restart, TTL expiry), the fallback uses `COUNT(*)` and caches the result — but the counter in the chat service's own flow is incremented independently. This means the counter can drift from the actual DB count, causing milestone checks to fire at wrong times (e.g., milestone at 100 messages might fire at 95 or 105).
**Fix:** Periodically reconcile Redis counters with actual DB counts, or use Redis `INCR` atomically alongside DB writes in a transaction with a post-commit hook.

### BUG-13: Missing Zod Validation on DM Controller
**File:** `server/src/modules/dm/dm.controller.ts`
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** Unlike other controllers, the DM controller has no Zod schema validation. The `sendMessage` method directly reads `content` and `messageType` from `req.body` without validation. A malicious client could send non-string content, extremely long strings, or invalid message types that could cause downstream issues.
**Fix:** Add Zod schemas for all DM controller endpoints matching the service's expectations.

### BUG-14: Missing Input Sanitization in Character Fact Keys
**Files:** `server/src/modules/character/facts.controller.ts` (addFact), `server/src/modules/character/character.service.ts` (addFact)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `addFact` endpoint in facts.controller.ts accepts user-defined `key` values up to 100 characters without normalization. The `character.service.ts` `addFact` method also doesn't normalize the key. However, the AI service's inline fact extraction normalizes keys to `snake_case`. This inconsistency means the same logical fact could be stored under different keys (e.g., `"user's favorite color"` vs `"user_s_favorite_color"`), causing duplicate/conflicting facts.
**Fix:** Normalize fact keys in the service layer using the same normalization function used by the AI inline fact extraction.

### BUG-15: Incorrect HTTP Status Code for Registration
**File:** `server/src/modules/auth/auth.controller.ts` (register)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `register` endpoint returns `200 OK` when it should return `202 Accepted` or `201 Created`. The OTP has been sent but the user account isn't created yet (that happens in `verifyRegistration`). Returning 200 is misleading as it implies the operation is complete.
**Fix:** Return `202 Accepted` with a message indicating OTP verification is pending.

### BUG-16: Missing `characterId` Ownership Check in Scene Service
**File:** `server/src/modules/scene/scene.service.ts` (unlockScene, setActiveScene)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** While the service checks that the user owns the active character (`prisma.character.findFirst({ where: { userId, isActive: true } })`), it then uses `character.id` for scene operations. If a user somehow has no active character, the `!character` check catches it. However, if a user has multiple characters (premium users), only the `isActive: true` one is checked — but scene unlocks are tied to a specific `characterId`. If the user deactivates their current character and activates another, the previously unlocked scenes don't transfer, but there's no user feedback about this.
**Fix:** This is more a UX issue than a security issue, but consider documenting this behavior or adding a warning.

### BUG-17: Potential XSS via Unescaped User Content in Email Templates
**File:** `server/src/lib/email.ts`
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The email templates interpolate the `otp` variable directly into HTML: `${otp}`. While OTPs are generated as numeric strings (6 digits), if the OTP generation logic ever changes to include user-controlled input, it would be an XSS vector. Currently safe due to `crypto.randomInt(100000, 999999).toString()`, but the pattern is fragile.
**Fix:** Use HTML entity encoding for all interpolated values in email templates, or use a template engine that auto-escapes.

### BUG-18: Race Condition in Daily Quest Auto-Start
**File:** `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` (autoStartDailyQuests)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The debounce uses a Redis key with TTL until midnight. If multiple server instances are running (horizontal scaling), the Redis debounce works correctly. However, the `deleteMany` + `create` operations happen in a transaction that doesn't account for the race where one instance deletes old progress and another instance is simultaneously incrementing progress on those same quests. The deleted progress is lost.
**Fix:** Only delete quests that are expired (started before yesterday), not just "before today". Or use an upsert pattern.

### BUG-19: Missing Error Response Format Consistency
**Files:** Multiple controllers
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** Some controllers return `{ success: false, error: { code, message } }` format (via AppError + error middleware), while others return `{ success: false, message: string }` directly (e.g., `password-reset.controller.ts`). This inconsistency makes it harder for frontend clients to handle errors uniformly.
**Specific files:**
- `password-reset.controller.ts` — returns `{ success: false, message: ... }` directly
- `auth.controller.ts` — uses AppError (correct pattern)
- `dm.controller.ts` — throws AppError (correct)
**Fix:** Standardize all error responses through the AppError + error middleware pattern.

### BUG-20: `getActiveCharacter` in Chat Controller Doesn't Validate Character ID
**File:** `server/src/modules/chat/chat.controller.ts` (getHistory)
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `getHistory` endpoint silently returns an empty message list if the user has no active character, rather than returning a 404 or a proper "no character" error. This could confuse clients that expect an error when no character exists.
**Fix:** Return a proper error response or at least include a flag in the response indicating no active character exists.

---

## LOW SEVERITY BUGS

### BUG-21: Hardcoded Redis URL Falls Back Without Warning
**File:** `server/src/lib/redis.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** If `REDIS_URL` is not set, the code falls back to `redis://localhost:6379` silently. In a Docker compose or production environment where Redis is on a different host, this causes silent connection failures. The warning log only fires if the connection fails, not when the default is used.
**Fix:** Log a warning when falling back to the default Redis URL.

### BUG-22: Unused `isVipTier` Import in Premium Middleware
**File:** `server/src/middlewares/premium.middleware.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The file imports `isVipTier` from `../lib/constants` but the function is also defined in `constants.ts` with a different implementation. The imported version checks `tier !== 'FREE'`, which is correct, but the duplication creates maintenance confusion.

### BUG-23: Magic Number in Socket Timeout
**File:** `server/src/sockets/index.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The AI response typing delay uses `setTimeout(() => {...}, 1000 + Math.random() * 2000)` — the 1000-3000ms range is hardcoded. The constants file has `TIMINGS.AI_TYPING_DELAY = 1500` but it's not used here.
**Fix:** Use the constant value or add min/max timing constants.

### BUG-24: Missing `distinct` Support May Cause Incorrect Leaderboard Results
**File:** `server/src/modules/leaderboard/leaderboard.service.ts` (getByLevel, getByAffection)
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The `distinct: ['userId']` in Prisma queries with `take: limit` may not work as expected on PostgreSQL when combined with `orderBy`. Prisma's `distinct` with ordering on different columns can produce unexpected results — it may return more than `limit` rows or skip legitimate top entries.
**Fix:** Use a subquery or raw SQL to get the best character per user first, then order and limit.

### BUG-25: `healthCache` Never Expires in Memory
**File:** `server/src/index.ts` (health endpoint)
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The health check uses an in-memory `healthCache` variable with a 5-second TTL. This works correctly but if the server runs for months without restart, this object persists indefinitely (albeit small). More importantly, in a multi-instance setup behind a load balancer, each instance maintains its own cache, causing inconsistent health responses.
**Fix:** Not a real issue for single-instance, but worth noting for horizontal scaling.

### BUG-26: Prisma Client Global Reuse in Development Could Mask Connection Issues
**File:** `server/src/lib/prisma.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The `global.prisma` pattern is correct for hot-reloading in development, but the connection logging (`prisma.$connect().then(...).catch(...)`) runs on every module reload in dev mode, potentially creating multiple connection pools. In dev mode with hot-reload, this could exhaust the database connection pool.
**Fix:** Only call `$connect()` explicitly; Prisma auto-connects on first query. Or guard the connection attempt.

### BUG-27: Missing `isActive` Filter in Gift Service's `sendGift`
**File:** `server/src/modules/gift/gift.service.ts` (sendGift)
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The `sendGift` method checks character ownership with `prisma.character.findFirst({ where: { id: data.characterId, userId } })` but doesn't filter by `isActive: true`. A user could theoretically send a gift to an ended/inactive character, which would still create gift history and AI responses.
**Fix:** Add `isActive: true` to the character ownership check.

### BUG-28: `updateCharacter` in Character Controller Doesn't Validate Response Style/Creativity Level Values
**File:** `server/src/modules/character/character.controller.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The `updateCharacterSchema` allows `responseStyle` as any string and `creativityLevel` as any number 0-1. However, the Character model's `responseStyle` has a default of `"romantic"` with no enum constraint, and `creativityLevel` is a Float. While technically valid, accepting arbitrary strings for `responseStyle` could cause issues if the AI service expects specific values.
**Fix:** Add enum validation for `responseStyle` matching the values the AI service actually uses.

### BUG-29: `getSubscriptionStatus` Makes Two Separate Queries When One Would Suffice
**File:** `server/src/modules/payment/payment.service.ts`
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** `getSubscriptionStatus` queries `prisma.subscription` and `prisma.user` separately. These could be combined or the user query could include the subscription relation.
**Fix:** Use `prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { id: userId }, include: { subscription: true } })` to fetch both in one query.

### BUG-30: Graceful Shutdown Doesn't Drain Socket Connections
**File:** `server/src/index.ts` (SIGTERM handler)
**Severity:** Low
**Description:** The SIGTERM handler closes the HTTP server and disconnects DB/Redis but doesn't gracefully drain active Socket.IO connections. Connected clients will receive abrupt disconnect errors rather than a proper shutdown notification.
**Fix:** Before `httpServer.close()`, emit a `server:shutting_down` event to all connected sockets, then wait a few seconds for clients to handle it gracefully.

## SCHEMA ISSUES

### BUG-31: Missing Index on `DirectMessage.conversationId` for JOIN Performance
**File:** `server/prisma/schema.prisma`
**Severity:** Medium
**Description:** The `DirectMessage` model has `@@index([conversationId, createdAt])` and `@@index([conversationId, isDeleted, createdAt])`, which is good. However, the `unreadCount` query in `dmService.getConversations` joins `conversation_members` with `direct_messages` on `conversationId` with additional filters. ## E2E Test Suite — Comprehensive Analysis Report

### Test Results Overview (Last Run: Chromium only, 2026-02-04)

| Metric | Count |
|--------|-------|
| **Total tests** | 40 (chromium project) |
| **Passed** | 27 |
| **Failed** | 6 |
| **Skipped** | 5 |
| **Interrupted** | 2 |
| **API tests executed** | 0 (not run in this session) |

Run status: **interrupted** — the test run was cut short, likely because the `should handle long messages` test's `beforeEach` login caused a browser context closure.

---

### Failed Tests (6 failures, all critical)

#### 1. `password-reset.spec.ts` — "should navigate back to login"
- **Error**: `expect(getCurrentUrl()).toContain('/login')` — URL stayed at `/auth/forgot-password`
- **Root cause**: `goToLogin()` calls `backToLoginLink.click()` but the link locator uses `a[href="/auth/login"], a:has-text("Quay lại")`. The "Quay lại" (Go back) link likely navigates back via browser history (`router.back()`) rather than a direct href to `/auth/login`, or the link doesn't exist/navigate properly.
- **Severity**: Medium — false positive or actual UX bug

#### 2. `chat.spec.ts` — "should send a message"
- **Error**: `TimeoutError: locator.click: Timeout 15000ms` on `sendButton` — element was **detached from DOM** during retry
- **Root cause**: The `sendButton` locator (`button:has(svg.lucide-send), button svg.lucide-send').locator('..').first()`) resolves to a `<div>` wrapper, not a `<button>`. The actual send button is likely being re-rendered by React, causing DOM detachment. The `.first()` on a parent traversal is fragile.
- **Severity**: **Critical** — cascades to 5+ other chat tests

#### 3. `chat.spec.ts` — "should receive AI response @regression"
- **Error**: Same `sendButton` DOM detachment timeout (same root cause as #2)
- **Severity**: **Critical**

#### 4. `chat.spec.ts` — "should show typing indicator while waiting"
- **Error**: `TimeoutError: locator.clear: Timeout 15000ms` on `messageInput` — the complex locator `input[placeholder*="Nhắn tin"], input[placeholder*="message" i], input[type="text"]:near(button:has(svg))` cannot find the element
- **Root cause**: The `:near()` pseudo-selector is **not supported** by Playwright's default locator engine. It requires the `internal:role` or CSS-only selectors. This locator will never match.
- **Severity**: **Critical** — the `messageInput` locator is broken

#### 5. `chat.spec.ts` — "should persist message history after refresh"
- **Error**: `TimeoutError: locator.waitFor: Timeout 15000ms` — same broken `messageInput` locator in `waitForPageLoad()`
- **Root cause**: Same as #4
- **Severity**: **Critical**

#### 6. `chat.spec.ts` — "should not send empty message"
- **Error**: Same `messageInput` locator timeout in `waitForPageLoad()`
- **Root cause**: Same as #4
- **Severity**: **Critical**

---

### Interrupted Tests (2)

#### `chat.spec.ts` — "should handle long messages" & "should be responsive on mobile"
- **Error**: `page.waitForURL: Target page, context or browser has been closed`
- **Root cause**: A preceding test failure in the same `describe` block (shared `beforeEach`) caused cascading browser context instability. The `beforeEach` login at `LoginPage.loginAndWaitForDashboard()` was waiting for navigation when the context was already torn down.
- **Severity**: High — test isolation issue

---

### Skipped Tests (5 skipped, all intentional)

All in `password-reset.spec.ts`:
- `should display OTP input fields`
- `should validate OTP format`
- `should show error for invalid OTP`
- `should have resend OTP option`
- `should complete full password reset flow`

**Reason**: These require real email/OTP integration which isn't mocked. This is acceptable, but they should be tracked as technical debt.

---

### Critical Issues Found

#### Issue 1: Broken `messageInput` locator in `ChatPage.ts` (line 56)
```typescript
this.messageInput = page.locator('input[placeholder*="Nhắn tin"], input[placeholder*="message" i], input[type="text"]:near(button:has(svg))').first();
```
- The `:near()` pseudo-selector is **not a valid Playwright selector**. Playwright uses CSS selectors + custom `internal:` selectors.
- This causes **5 test failures** and makes the entire Chat test suite unreliable.
- **Fix**: Replace with a simpler, reliable selector like `page.getByRole('textbox', { name: /message|nhắn/i })` or `page.locator('textarea, input[type="text"]').first()`.

#### Issue 2: Fragile `sendButton` locator in `ChatPage.ts` (line 57)
```typescript
this.sendButton = page.locator('button:has(svg.lucide-send), button svg.lucide-send').locator('..').first();
```
- The second part `button svg.lucide-send` selects an `<svg>`, then `.locator('..')` goes to parent — which may be a `<div>`, not a `<button>`.
- React re-renders cause DOM node detachment, making click retries fail.
- **Fix**: Use `page.getByRole('button', { name: /send|gửi/i })` or `page.locator('button[data-testid="send-button"], button:has(svg.lucide-send)')`.

#### Issue 3: No test isolation in Chat `describe` block
The `beforeEach` in `chat.spec.ts` performs a full login for every test. When one test fails with a timeout, the shared page context is corrupted, causing cascading failures and even browser context closure.
- **Fix**: Use `test.describe.serial` for the Chat suite, or ensure each test gets a fresh context via fixtures.

#### Issue 4: `forgotPasswordPage.goToLogin()` navigation assertion is unreliable
The `backToLoginLink` uses `a:has-text("Quay lại")` which matches a "go back" link that may use `router.back()` instead of navigating to `/auth/login`.

#### Issue 5: `BasePage.fillInput()` uses `locator.clear()` then `locator.fill()`
`locator.clear()` is deprecated in newer Playwright versions. `locator.fill()` already clears the input automatically. The extra `.clear()` adds an unnecessary step that can timeout.

#### Issue 6: Password Reset tests have no assertions in several tests
Several tests in `password-reset.spec.ts` (e.g., "should show error for non-existent email", "should validate password requirements") have **no assertions** — they just wait and do nothing. These are effectively no-op tests that always pass.

| **Auth UI (Register)** | ✅ Covered | 8 tests, all passing |

| **Character API** | Not executed in last run | Needs manual run |
| **Chat API** | Not executed in last run | Needs manual run |
| **Quest/Gift/Scene/Memory API** | Not executed in last run | Needs manual run |
| **Mobile View** | ❌ Not executed | Only 1 mobile test, interrupted |
| **Multi-browser** | ❌ Not executed | Firefox/WebKit/Safari projects not run |
| **DM/Messaging** | ❌ Missing | No tests for user-to-user DM feature |
| **Quests UI** | ❌ Missing | No UI tests for quest system |
| **Shop/Gifts UI** | ❌ Missing | No UI tests for shop/gift features |
| **Leaderboard/Ranking** | ❌ Missing | No tests for ranking system |
| **Error boundaries** | ❌ Missing | No tests for app crash recovery |
| **Accessibility** | ❌ Missing | Only helper exists, no tests use it |

---

### Architectural Concerns

1. **No retries configured**: `retries: 0` in the test run (from `.last-run.json`). Config says `RETRY_COUNT=2` but it wasn't applied.
2. **`workers: 2` with `fullyParallel: true`**: Tests that share state (e.g., creating users with the same email pattern) can collide.
3. **Test data cleanup missing**: `globalTeardown.ts` is a stub — no test users or data are cleaned up. Over time, the test database will accumulate stale data.
4. **No API tests in this run**: The `api` project wasn't executed — either the run was `--project=chromium` only, or the API tests weren't triggered.
5. **No CI integration visible**: Tests rely on local dev servers (`webServer` in config), but no CI pipeline validation.

---

### Recommendations (Prioritized)

**P0 — Fix immediately:**
1. **Fix `messageInput` locator** in `ChatPage.ts` — remove `:near()` pseudo-selector, use `getByRole('textbox')` or simpler CSS
2. **Fix `sendButton` locator** in `ChatPage.ts` — use `getByRole('button', { name: /send/i })` or data-testid
3. **Re-run Chat tests** after fixes — expect 5+ tests to pass

**P1 — High priority:**
4. **Add assertions to password-reset tests** that currently have none (lines 23-33, 114-137)
5. **Fix `goToLogin()` assertion** in `password-reset.spec.ts` — use `waitForURL` instead of checking `getCurrentUrl()` after click
6. **Remove deprecated `locator.clear()`** from `BasePage.fillInput()` — `fill()` already clears
7. **Add test data cleanup** in `globalTeardown.ts` — delete test users created during runs

**P2 — Medium priority:**
8. **Run full test suite** including `api` project to validate API coverage
9. **Add `test.describe.serial`** to Chat test suite to prevent shared-state corruption
10. **Un-skip OTP tests** by mocking the email service or using test API to retrieve OTPs
11. **Add DM/Messaging E2E tests** — this is a listed feature with zero coverage
12. **Add Quest/Shop UI tests** — gamification features listed but untested

**P3 — Low priority / Nice to have:**
13. **Add accessibility tests** using the existing `checkBasicAccessibility` helper
14. **Add visual regression tests** for key pages (dashboard, chat)
15. **Run cross-browser tests** (Firefox, WebKit, Mobile) in CI
16. **Add performance budgets** — track test execution time trends

---

### Unresolved Questions

1. **Why was the test run interrupted?** Was it manually stopped, or did it hit a resource limit/timeout?
2. **Are API tests passing?** They weren't executed in the last run — need a dedicated `npm run test:api` execution.
3. **What is the actual chat input element in the app?** The current locator assumes an `<input type="text">` with Vietnamese placeholder "Nhắn tin...", but the app may use a `<textarea>` or different structure.
4. **Is the send button actually a `<button>` or a `<div>` styled as a button?** The error shows it resolved to `<div class="absolute right-2 top-1/2...">` — need to verify actual DOM structure.
5. **Does the "Quay lại" (go back) link on forgot-password page use `router.back()` or `href="/auth/login"`?** This affects whether the test assertion is correct or needs updating.

I now have enough data for a comprehensive review. Let me compile the report.

---

## Code Review Summary — VirtualGfriend

### Scope
- **Server files reviewed**: `index.ts`, sockets, auth, chat, payment, gift, game-event, character, AI, DM services, middleware (auth, error, premium), routes
- **Client files reviewed**: socket.ts, api.ts, auth-store, chat-store, chat/page.tsx, PremiumGate.tsx, use-auth-guard
- **Focus**: Full-stack quality, security, performance, edge cases

---

## CRITICAL (Fix Immediately)

### C1. Race Condition: Gift Double-Spend (No Transaction Isolation)
**File**: `server/src/modules/gift/gift.service.ts` — `sendGift()`

The inventory check (`userGift.quantity < 1`) happens **before** the transaction. Two concurrent `sendGift` calls can both pass the check, then both decrement, resulting in negative quantity or double-gifting.

```ts
// Check inventory — NOT in transaction
const userGift = await prisma.userGift.findUnique({
  where: { userId_giftId: { userId, giftId: data.giftId } },
  include: { gift: true },
});
if (!userGift || userGift.quantity < 1) {
  throw new AppError('Gift not in inventory', 400, 'GIFT_NOT_OWNED');
}
// Then later in transaction:
await tx.userGift.update({
  data: { quantity: { decrement: 1 } },  // Can go negative!
});
```

**Fix**: Move the quantity check **inside** the transaction, or use Prisma's `@idempotent` constraint / atomic update with a `where: { quantity: { gte: 1 } }` guard.

### C2. JWT_SECRET Used Directly — No Key Rotation
**File**: `server/src/sockets/index.ts:57`, `server/src/middlewares/auth.middleware.ts:72`

All JWT verification uses `process.env.JWT_SECRET!` directly with no algorithm restriction. An attacker who controls the `alg` header could downgrade to `none` or `HS256` with a public key.

```ts
const decoded = jwt.verify(token, process.env.JWT_SECRET!) as JwtPayload;
```

**Fix**: Pass `{ algorithms: ['HS256'] }` to every `jwt.verify()` call.

### C3. Stripe Webhook — Raw Body Parsing Fragility
**File**: `server/src/index.ts:73`

The webhook route uses `express.raw({ type: 'application/json' })` which is correct, but if any middleware before it (e.g., `express.json()`) is reordered, signature verification breaks silently. Currently `helmet()` and `compression()` come before it — safe, but `cookie-parser` comes after the webhook mount which is fine. However, the route is defined **before** `express.json()` which is correct but fragile.

**Risk**: If anyone adds middleware above the webhook mount that parses the body, Stripe webhook will break.

**Fix**: Add a comment warning and consider isolating the webhook on a separate Express app instance.

### C4. Token Refresh Race — Stale Token in Zustand Store
**File**: `client/src/services/api.ts` — `doRefreshToken()`

When `doRefreshToken()` succeeds, it writes the new token to `localStorage` directly, but the **Zustand store** (`useAuthStore`) is not notified. The in-memory `accessToken` in Zustand becomes stale until the next render/rehydration. Subsequent API calls in the same tick may use the old token.

**Fix**: After updating localStorage, call `useAuthStore.getState().setAccessToken(newToken)` to sync the in-memory state.

### C5. Unbounded `Promise.all` in `autoClaimQuest`
**File**: `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` — `processAction()` line ~90

```ts
await Promise.all(
  result.questsCompleted.map((completed) => this.autoClaimQuest(userId, completed.questId, _depth))
);
```

Each `autoClaimQuest` calls `processAction` recursively (for `COMPLETE_QUEST`). If 5 quests complete simultaneously, this spawns 5 parallel DB transactions + 5 recursive `processAction` chains. With enough completions this can overwhelm the DB connection pool.

**Fix**: Process sequentially or add a concurrency limiter (e.g., `p-limit`).

---

## HIGH (Fix Soon)

### H1. `isEvening` Check Is Off-By-One
**File**: `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` line 145

```ts
const isEvening = currentHour >= 21 && currentHour <= 24;
```

`getHours()` returns 0-23, so `<= 24` is always true. The effective check is `>= 21`. Should be `currentHour >= 21 && currentHour < 24` or simply `currentHour >= 21`.

### H2. Chat Store — `replaceMessage` Uses Temp ID That May Not Match
**File**: `client/src/store/chat-store.ts` — `replaceMessage()`

The optimistic message uses `clientId` as its `id`. When `replaceMessage` is called, it looks for `tempId`. But the socket handler in `socket.ts` receives `clientId` and uses it to find the temp message. If the server echoes back a message with a **different** `id` than `clientId`, the replacement fails silently and a duplicate message appears.

**Fix**: Ensure the server echo includes the original `clientId` as a lookup key, or use a two-key match (match `clientId` in metadata, then replace).

### H3. Missing Authorization on `updateAffection` and `addExperience`
**File**: `server/src/modules/character/character.service.ts`

`updateAffection(characterId, amount, userId?)` — the `userId` param is **optional**. When called from `chatService.sendMessage()` and `giftService.sendGift()`, `userId` is **not passed**:

```ts
// In chat.service.ts:
await characterService.updateAffection(character.id, aiResponse.affectionChange);
// No userId! Authorization check is skipped.
```

This means if any endpoint leaks a `characterId`, any authenticated user can manipulate another user's character affection.

**Fix**: Always pass `userId` and make the parameter required.

### H4. Redis Milestone Counters Are Never Initialized
**File**: `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` lines 96-107

```ts
if (action === 'SEND_MESSAGE') {
  const key = `milestone_msg_count:${userId}`;
  const current = await cache.get<number>(key);
  if (current !== null) {  // ← Only increments if already exists
    await cache.set(key, current + 1, 3600);
  }
}
```

The counter is never initialized to 0, so the first `SEND_MESSAGE` does nothing (`current === null`). This means milestone checks for `messageCount === 1` will **never fire** because the counter stays `null` and falls back to the expensive DB COUNT.

**Fix**: Initialize with `cache.setNX(key, 1, 3600)` on first message, or use `INCR`.

### H5. `updateUserStats` Recursion Can Trigger on Every Action
**File**: `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` — `updateUserStats()`

When a new day is detected, `processAction` is called with `DAILY_LOGIN` and `MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH - 1` (=2). But `DAILY_LOGIN` itself triggers `autoStartDailyQuests`, which does DB queries. Combined with the outer `processAction` call, this creates a nested chain of DB operations on what should be a simple message send.

**Fix**: Fire `DAILY_LOGIN` as a fire-and-forget background task instead of recursive `processAction`.

### H6. No Input Sanitization on AI Service User Message
**File**: `server/src/modules/chat/chat.service.ts` — `sendMessage()`

User message content is passed directly into the AI system prompt and recent messages context. While the AI service has a "forbidden topics" guard in the system prompt, there's no server-side sanitization. A crafted message could attempt prompt injection to bypass character constraints.

**Fix**: Add server-side content filtering for known injection patterns, and validate the AI response JSON structure before trusting `affection_change` or `moodChange`.

### H7. `chat-store.ts` Persists Messages to localStorage — XSS Risk
**File**: `client/src/store/chat-store.ts`

Messages (including AI-generated content) are persisted to `localStorage` via `persist` middleware. If any AI response contains malicious HTML/script and the app renders it without proper escaping, it becomes a stored XSS vector.

Currently the chat page renders with `{message.content}` which is safe (React escapes by default), but any future `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` usage would be dangerous.

**Fix**: Document this risk and ensure no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` is ever used with message content.

### H8. Payment Webhook — No Idempotency for `checkout.session.completed`
**File**: `server/src/modules/payment/payment.service.ts` — `handleCheckoutCompleted()`

If Stripe retries the webhook (which it does for up to 3 days), the same session can trigger multiple `upsert` operations. The `upsert` partially mitigates this, but the `user.update` for premium activation runs on **every** retry, which is wasteful and can cause cache invalidation storms.

**Fix**: Check if the subscription already exists with status `ACTIVE` before doing the user update.

---

## MEDIUM (Fix When Convenient)

### M1. `chat-store.ts` — `mergeMessages` Can Lose Ordering
**File**: `client/src/store/chat-store.ts` — `mergeMessages()`

The merge uses a `Map` keyed by `message.id`. If two messages have the same `id` but different content (e.g., updated on server), the first one wins and the server update is lost.

**Fix**: Use the newer `createdAt` timestamp to decide which version wins.

### M2. `api.ts` — 429 Retry Uses Arbitrary 5-Second Cap
**File**: `client/src/services/api.ts`

```ts
await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, Math.min(waitMs, 5000)));
```

If the server says `Retry-After: 30`, the client only waits 5 seconds and retries anyway, which could compound the rate limiting issue.

**Fix**: Respect the full `Retry-After` header, or at least cap at 10s.

### M3. Socket Service — External Listener List Grows Unbounded
**File**: `client/src/services/socket.ts` — `on()` method

```ts
on<T extends unknown[]>(event: string, callback: (...args: T) => void) {
  this.externalListeners.push({ event, callback: callback as EventCallback })
  this.socket?.on(event, callback as EventCallback)
}
```

Every call to `on()` adds to `externalListeners`. If components call `on()` on every render without proper cleanup via `off()`, the array grows and duplicate listeners accumulate.

**Fix**: Deduplicate before pushing, or use a WeakMap pattern.

### M4. `PremiumGate` — Tier Configs Fetched But Not Awaited
**File**: `client/src/components/PremiumGate.tsx`

The `useEffect` calls `fetchTierConfigs()` but the access check runs immediately with potentially stale `allTierConfigs`. On first render, `configs` falls back to `PREMIUM_FEATURES` (hardcoded), which may differ from server config.

**Fix**: Show a loading state while configs are being fetched for the first time.

### M5. `chat.controller.ts` — `getHistory` Returns Empty on No Character
**File**: `server/src/modules/chat/chat.controller.ts` — `getHistory()`

When no character exists, it returns `messages: []` with a 200 status. This is semantically correct but masks the real issue — the user should have been redirected to character creation.

**Fix**: Return a specific code like `NO_CHARACTER` so the client can redirect.

### M6. `auth-store.ts` — `partialize` Only Persists Token, Not User
**File**: `client/src/store/auth-store.ts`

```ts
partialize: (state) => ({
  accessToken: state.accessToken,
}),
```

On page refresh, the `user` object is `null` until `checkAuth()` completes. This causes a flash of "not authenticated" state even though the token is valid.

**Fix**: Also persist `user` in `partialize`, or add a `isRehydrating` state.

### M7. `game-event.service.ts` — `giveMilestoneReward` Not in Transaction
**File**: `server/src/modules/game/game-event.service.ts` — `giveMilestoneReward()`

The user balance update and character affection update are two separate DB calls. If the second fails, the user gets coins but no affection.

**Fix**: Wrap in a `prisma.$transaction()`.

### M8. `character.service.ts` — `RELATIONSHIP_THRESHOLDS` Inconsistency
**File**: `server/src/modules/character/character.service.ts`

The thresholds have `IN_LOVE: 850` and `LOVER: 900`, but `calculateRelationshipStage` checks `>= 900` for `LOVER` and `>= 750` for `DATING` — it **skips** `IN_LOVE` entirely. The stage `IN_LOVE` is unreachable.

```ts
if (affection >= 900) return 'LOVER';
if (affection >= 750) return 'DATING';  // Catches 750-899, IN_LOVE never reached
```

**Fix**: Add `if (affection >= 850) return 'IN_LOVE';` between the two checks.

---

## LOW (Nice to Have)

### L1. `console.log` in Production Auth Code
**File**: `server/src/modules/auth/auth.service.ts` — `login()`

```ts
console.log('[Auth] Login attempt:', { email: data.email, userFound: !!user });
console.log('[Auth] Password check:', { isValid: isValidPassword });
```

These leak email addresses to server logs. Use the structured logger instead.

### L2. `chat-store.ts` — `Date` Serialization Risk
**File**: `client/src/store/chat-store.ts`

Messages have `createdAt: Date | string`. When persisted to localStorage, Dates become strings. On rehydration, `sort()` compares strings which works for ISO format, but any downstream code expecting `Date` objects will break.

### L3. `socket.ts` — `on('error', () => {})` Swallows Errors Silently
**File**: `client/src/services/socket.ts`

```ts
this.socket.on('error', () => {
  // Socket error handled silently
});
```

Silent error handler hides connection failures from users.

### L4. `chat/page.tsx` — 608+ Lines, Should Be Split
The chat page component exceeds 600 lines mixing message rendering, gift modal, scene selector, socket setup, and i18n. Should extract sub-components.

### L5. `ai.service.ts` — System Prompt Is ~600 Lines of Hardcoded String
The `buildSystemPrompt` function constructs a massive system prompt as a template literal. This should be extracted to a config file or template system for easier maintenance.

### L6. No CSRF Protection
The app uses `credentials: 'include'` with cookies for refresh tokens but has no CSRF protection middleware. The `sameSite: 'strict'` cookie flag mitigates this, but explicit CSRF tokens would be defense-in-depth.

### L7. `error.middleware.ts` — `(req as any).user` Type Cast
**File**: `server/src/middlewares/error.middleware.ts`

```ts
userId: (req as any).user?.id,
```

Should use the properly typed `req.user` from the Express type augmentation in `auth.middleware.ts`.

---

## Recommended Actions (Prioritized)

| # | Issue | Severity | Effort |
|---|-------|----------|--------|
| 1 | Gift double-spend race condition | **CRITICAL** | Low |
| 2 | JWT `algorithms` restriction | **CRITICAL** | Low |
| 3 | Zustand store stale after token refresh | **CRITICAL** | Low |
| 4 | `updateAffection` missing userId auth check | **HIGH** | Low |
| 5 | Redis milestone counters never initialized | **HIGH** | Low |
| 6 | `isEvening` off-by-one bug | **HIGH** | Trivial |
| 7 | `IN_LOVE` relationship stage unreachable | **MEDIUM** | Trivial |
| 8 | `giveMilestoneReward` not transactional | **MEDIUM** | Low |
| 9 | `console.log` in auth service (info leak) | **LOW** | Trivial |
| 10 | Stripe webhook idempotency | **HIGH** | Low |
